message:
feat(core,parser,render,layout): add node icon extraction, custom SVG icons, and left-position layout

Parser: extract fa: and emoji icon prefixes from node labels across all
diagram types (flowchart, mindmap, ER, class, packet). New helpers
extract_icon_prefix, split_fontawesome_icon_prefix, split_emoji_icon_prefix
parse the icon token out of label text and propagate it through NodeToken
and FlowAstNode to the IR via intern_node_token/intern_flow_ast_node.

Render-SVG: add NodeIconPosition enum (Above/Left) and CustomSvgIcon
struct for user-supplied path-data icons. render_node now reserves
horizontal space for left-positioned icons and shifts the label center
accordingly. render_custom_svg_icon scales arbitrary path data into the
node icon slot. CSS classes fm-node-icon-pos-above/left emitted for
styling hooks.

WASM: wire nodeIconPosition and customIcons config overrides through
SvgConfigOverrides with validation and BTreeMap merge.

Layout: widen node bounding box when icon and label appear side-by-side
(label_width + icon_width * 0.85) to prevent clipping.

Refactor state guard/action tests to unit-test extract_state_guard_action
directly instead of round-tripping through full parse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-pdec","title":"Sequence parity: honor sequence.showSequenceNumbers init config","description":"Mermaid sequence diagrams support enabling message numbering through init/front-matter config via sequence.showSequenceNumbers. frankenmermaid already renders numbered messages when sequence_meta.autonumber is set, but the config path is not wired through. Add config parsing/storage and parser lowering so showSequenceNumbers enables autonumber deterministically, with focused tests for init JSON and front matter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:05:16.952952248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.315315840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.314720413Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","parity","parser","render","sequence"]}
 {"id":"bd-peow","title":"Implement click directive rendering with URL targets","description":"# Click Directive Rendering

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
click nodeA href "https://example.com" "Tooltip text"
click nodeA callback "functionName"
click nodeA href "https://example.com" _blank
\`\`\`
Nodes become clickable links. URLs open in new tab, callbacks invoke JS functions.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes click directives and extracts href/callback values
- IrNode has \`href: Option<String>\` field that stores the URL
- SVG renderer does NOT wrap nodes in <a> elements or add cursor styling
- URL sanitization IS implemented (blocks javascript:, vbscript: schemes)

## Implementation Plan
1. **SVG Renderer**: When IrNode.href is Some, wrap the node's <g> element in an SVG <a> element with xlink:href. Add cursor:pointer CSS. Add <title> for tooltip.
2. **Tests**: Verify <a> wrapping in SVG output. Verify tooltip. Verify sanitized URLs don't produce <a> elements.

## Security
- URL sanitization already in place — must verify it covers all attack vectors
- No callback support (WASM/JS only) — document as intentional limitation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.616386903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.933197674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.932777199Z","close_reason":"ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: SVG renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs lines 1671-1684 already wraps nodes in <a> elements with href, target=_blank, rel=noopener when IrNode.href is Some. Verified by code review. No work needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-peow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qiyv","title":"Make quadrant chart dimensions configurable","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded quadrant chart dimensions (chart_w=400, chart_h=400, margin_left=80, margin_top=60) with configurable or content-adaptive values.

## Why
Both the layout (layout_diagram_quadrant_traced) and SVG renderer (render_quadrant_svg) use identical hardcoded 400x400 chart dimensions. This means:
- The chart does not adapt to the available viewport/terminal size
- Long axis labels can overflow the margins
- The chart is always square even if the content suggests a different aspect ratio
- Terminal rendering at small sizes wastes most of the 400x400 area

## Proposed Approach
1. Add `chart_width` and `chart_height` fields to a `QuadrantLayoutConfig` (or reuse LayoutSpacing)
2. Compute margins from axis label dimensions using FontMetrics
3. In terminal renderer, scale to the actual cell dimensions instead of using fixed 400x400
4. Keep 400x400 as the default for backward compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quadrant charts render correctly at different terminal sizes
- Long axis labels do not overflow
- Default behavior unchanged for typical usage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:10.923449900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407968687Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407452109Z","close_reason":"Quadrant chart_w/chart_h now scale with point count: base=300+15*n, clamped [200,600]. Margin_left computed from axis label width. Square aspect preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-qj2b","title":"Implement state notes, guards, and transition actions","description":"# State Notes, Guards, and Actions

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active
  note right of Active: This state is active
  note left of Active
    Multi-line
    note content
  end note
  Active --> Done : complete [isValid] / cleanup()
  Active : entry: startTimer()
  Active : exit: stopTimer()
\`\`\`
- Notes: positioned left/right of states
- Guard conditions: [condition] on transitions
- Actions: / action() on transitions
- Entry/exit actions: declared as state descriptions

## Current State
- Notes: NOT parsed
- Guards/actions on transitions: NOT parsed (treated as part of label text)
- Entry/exit actions: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`note right of\`/\`note left of\` statements. Parse guard conditions in brackets and actions after / in transition labels. Parse \`StateName : entry: action\` / \`exit: action\`.
2. **IR**: Add notes, guard/action fields to edges, entry/exit to nodes.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes, show guard/action text on edges.
4. **Tests**: Notes, guarded transitions, entry/exit actions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.380064165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:37:41.850372887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","state"],"comments":[{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-qj2b","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

- cargo check --workspace --all-targets

4. Transition with action: A --> B : event / doSomething() → verify action renders
5. State with entry: A : entry: initialize() → verify entry action in state box

 {"id":"bd-qsr8","title":"Kanban: Implement card metadata, WIP limits, and priority styling","description":"# Kanban Rendering Improvements

## What mermaid-js Supports (v11+)
\`\`\`
kanban
  Todo
    id1[Design] @{ priority: high, assigned: alice }
    id2[Implement]
  In Progress @{ wip: 2 }
    id3[Testing]
  Done
    id4[Deploy]
\`\`\`
- Card metadata: priority, assigned, tags via @{} syntax
- Column WIP limits: @{ wip: N } on column headers
- Ticket base URL for linking cards to issue trackers
- Card styling based on priority/status

## Current State
- Columns and cards parsed
- @{} metadata syntax NOT parsed
- WIP limits NOT parsed or rendered
- No priority-based styling

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse @{key: value} syntax on cards and columns. Extract priority, assigned, tags, wip.
2. **IR**: Add metadata map to kanban card nodes and WIP limit to clusters.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Style cards by priority (high=red border, medium=yellow, low=green). Show WIP limit indicator on column headers. Show assigned name as badge.
4. **Tests**: Card metadata, WIP limits, priority styling.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.403662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.600150293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["kanban","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-qsr8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-r2yy","title":"Sequence: implement hide footbox parity","description":"# Mermaid sequence diagrams support `hide footbox`, which suppresses mirrored/footer participant boxes at the diagram bottom.

Implement parser support plus deterministic layout/render behavior across SVG and terminal surfaces.

Acceptance:

- SVG and term outputs match layout behavior

 {"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:56:03.963956918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Integration Tests

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zi0e","title":"Sequence: support named box colors and transparent box syntax parity","description":"# Sequence box color parity

## Goal
Support Mermaid sequence box declarations that use named colors and the special transparent form, matching current docs examples like `box Aqua Group Description` and `box transparent Aqua`.

## Scope
- Parse leading named CSS color tokens in sequence `box` declarations, not just hex/rgb/hsl forms.
- Treat `box transparent <label>` as transparent box fill with `<label>` preserved as the label.
- Preserve the parsed group color through sequence IR/layout to SVG rendering for participant group clusters.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `box Aqua Group` stores color `Aqua` and label `Group`.
- `box transparent Aqua` stores a transparent color and label `Aqua`.
- SVG uses the parsed group color without unsafe attribute injection.
- Quality gates for touched crates pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:41:43.328437882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.194234898Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.193746754Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence box color parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-zmbp","title":"State: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# State Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Basic states and transitions work (~75% parser). Missing all UML pseudostate types that make state diagrams expressive.

## Gaps
1. Start/end pseudo-state special rendering ([*])
2. Fork/join parallel bars
3. Choice pseudo-state (diamond shape)
4. History states (H / H*)
5. Composite/nested states with proper containment
6. Notes on states
7. Entry/exit actions on transitions
8. Guard conditions [guard] on transitions
9. State descriptions (state name : description)
10. Concurrency regions (-- separator within composite state)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/stateDiagram.html","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:19.858400163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.981662394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:48.302936595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-qj2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.980550820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-zn9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.128081199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-zmbp","title":"State: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# State Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Basic states and transitions work (~75% parser). Missing all UML pseudostate types that make state diagrams expressive.

## Gaps
1. Start/end pseudo-state special rendering ([*])
2. Fork/join parallel bars
3. Choice pseudo-state (diamond shape)
4. History states (H / H*)
5. Composite/nested states with proper containment
6. Notes on states
7. Entry/exit actions on transitions
8. Guard conditions [guard] on transitions
9. State descriptions (state name : description)
10. Concurrency regions (-- separator within composite state)

## Reference

 {"id":"bd-zn9n","title":"Implement composite/nested states with containment and concurrency","description":"# Composite/Nested States and Concurrency

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active {
    [*] --> Processing
    Processing --> Done
    --
    [*] --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> Alert
  }
  Idle --> Active
  Active --> Idle
\`\`\`
- States can contain child states (composite state)
- \`--\` separator creates concurrent regions within a composite state
- Each concurrent region has independent start/end states
- Nesting can be arbitrarily deep

## Current State
- Composite state syntax (\`state Name { ... }\`) IS parsed — creates subgraphs
- Concurrency separator \`--\` is NOT parsed
- Layout does not enforce visual containment of child states
- SVG renderer draws clusters but doesn't render concurrency dividers

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`--\` within composite state blocks as region separators. Each region becomes a separate sub-cluster within the parent cluster.
2. **Layout**: Ensure cluster bounds fully contain all child node bounds. For concurrent regions, split the cluster horizontally with a dashed divider.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw composite states with rounded rectangle borders. Draw dashed horizontal line for concurrency dividers. Add region labels if present.
4. **Tests**: Nested states, three levels deep, concurrent regions, transitions crossing region boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.201073543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:12:15.275149600Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:12:15.274637351Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:31.377491237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":153,"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
